article_meta.title: Supreme Court ruling against Trump’s tariffs is unlikely to mean an end to trade policy chaos 1 of 3 | Containers are st

reasoning: Good identification of multiple loaded phrases in the author’s own prose with no attribution violations, but it missed several clear instances of author-level spin and loaded wording.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged items are from the author’s narrative, not from quoted or clearly attributed sources.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Each flagged phrase reflects interpretive or value-laden language in the author’s voice (e.g., ‘stunning rebuke,’ ‘strong-armed,’ ‘chaotic’).
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed notable biased phrases such as ‘conjure up new import taxes on a whim’ and ‘a handy cudgel with which to smack countries,’ and the subhead/phrasing ‘A messy refund process.’
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are broadly defensible as Spin, though some could be more precisely tagged as Word Choice or Subjective Qualifying Adjectives; still largely appropriate.
